event_blocks.summary: Tasos Georgiou, a 32-year-old professional Karagiozis (shadow theater) performer from Athens, introduced himself to the hosts on the brightly lit 'Ellada Exei Talento' stage, which featured a colorful, hand-painted theatrical backdrop depicting a parade of cartoonish figures under a red curtain with theatrical masks, later subtly changing to a castle silhouette. He explained his lifelong passion for the art form, having started professionally in sixth grade. As upbeat background music transitioned to a distinct Karagiozis-style melody, Tasos began his performance with theatrical vocalizations, presenting a Lenten song titled 'Fasolada' and engaging in comedic dialogue with shadow puppet characters Hadjiavatis and Karagiozis. The performance involved stylized shadow puppets, including one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs, another in a blue and gold costume with red boots (identified as Karagiozis), and a third in a green dress, interacting dynamically. The dialogue humorously touched upon food, Karagiozis's self-proclaimed charm, and his role in making people laugh and forget their problems. The judges, including the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer, the Female Judge with Platinum Hair, and the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt, reacted with visible amusement, laughter, and attentive observation, with the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt frequently covering his mouth. The audience responded with enthusiastic applause, cheering, and audible laughter throughout the lively and entertaining segment, which concluded with Karagiozis announcing the end of the performance and bidding farewell to the audience and judges.

Theatrical Backdrop.description: A large, hand-painted stage backdrop featuring cartoonish characters in a parade, including a donkey and figures in traditional attire, under a red curtain with theatrical masks; later updated to include a castle silhouette, suggesting a narrative element.

Female Judge with Platinum Hair.description: A woman with short platinum hair, wearing a black patterned top and large hoop earrings, seated at the judges' panel, smiling and laughing in response to the shadow puppet performance.

Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer.description: A man with a beard, wearing a grey checkered blazer over a white shirt, seated at the judges' panel, watching the performance with interest, resting his chin on his hand.

Tasos Georgiou.description: A large man with a beard, wearing a black button-up shirt and jeans, performing on stage in front of a colorful, hand-painted backdrop; identified by on-screen text as 'ΤΑΣΟΣ ΓΕΩΡΓΙΟΥ'. He introduces himself, states he is from Athens, confirms he performs Karagiozis, mentions he started professionally in sixth grade, and is 32 years old. He then performs distinct, high-pitched theatrical vocalizations as part of his act.

Male Judge in Black T-Shirt.description: A man with a beard, wearing a black t-shirt, seated at the judges' panel, reacting with amusement, at one point covering his mouth with his hand.

Audience Members.description: A diverse group of spectators in the audience, shown clapping and reacting positively to the performance, with their applause clearly audible under blue stage lighting.

Shadow Puppet Characters.description: Three stylized shadow puppets, including the central figure Karagiozis (likely the one in a blue and gold costume with red boots, who is the jester and speaks in Greek about making people laugh and forget their problems), another character addressed as Hadjiavatis (or Hadjiatzaris) who engages in dialogue in Greek, one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs, and a third in a green dress, all performing animated gestures and interactions on a white screen.

raw_segments: The 'Ellada Exei Talento' stage is brightly lit in blue, showcasing the judges' panel with prominent 'X' logos and a lively audience. Upbeat, rhythmic background music plays as a large man with a beard, wearing a black button-up shirt and jeans, identified by on-screen text as 'ΤΑΣΟΣ ΓΕΩΡΓΙΟΥ', walks onto the stage. He greets the hosts, introducing himself as Tasos Georgiou, and confirms he is from Athens, specifically Kypseli. He engages in a conversation with the hosts, revealing he will perform Karagiozis (shadow theater) and has been involved professionally since sixth grade, now being 32 years old. The judges, including a male judge in a grey checkered blazer, listen intently, nodding and showing amusement. The audience claps and reacts positively. Behind Tasos, a colorful, hand-painted backdrop depicting a whimsical parade of cartoonish figures under a red curtain with theatrical masks is visible, later subtly changing to a castle silhouette. As the hosts wish him well, the background music transitions to a distinct Karagiozis-style melody, and Tasos begins his act with high-pitched, theatrical vocalizations, prompting laughter and continued positive reactions from the audience and judges, with one male judge covering his mouth in amusement.

The segment opens with the audience, illuminated by blue stage lighting, clapping and reacting with visible enthusiasm, their applause and cheering audible as lively, traditional Greek-sounding music begins. A male voice exclaims "Opa!" before the scene transitions to a shadow puppet performance. Silhouetted, cartoonish figures move dynamically across a brightly lit screen, set against a hand-painted backdrop initially depicting a tree and a castle, suggesting a fairy-tale setting. The male voice continues with rhythmic, chant-like speech, including phrases like "Opa, opa, opa, opa, hey!" and "Για ρε γλεντια!" As the shadow figures dance and interact, with one large figure appearing to lead, the camera cuts to the judges’ panel. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer is seen laughing and covering his mouth in amusement, while the Female Judge with Platinum Hair observes with a composed, attentive expression. The performance maintains a lively, whimsical tone, with the shadow figures engaging in synchronized movements, accompanied by the male voice delivering theatrical lines such as "Ε ρε να μου ζήσεις ξυπόλητο τάγμα!" and the repeated, comedic "Μερσί Μαέστρο!" (Merci Maestro!). The Male Judge in Black T-Shirt is also shown reacting with amusement. The male voice continues with playful, self-referential dialogue, mentioning "Μερσί Μαέστρο μου, μερσί και η μύτη σου τουρσί" (Merci Maestro, merci and your nose is pickled). The backdrop is now clearly visible as a colorful, hand-painted scene featuring a parade of cartoonish figures under a red curtain with theatrical masks, adding to the narrative depth. The male voice then introduces the act: "Πολυτήρης Καλατζόπουλος, παρών! Τι παράσταση θα παρουσιάσουμε απόψε πουλάκι μου; Αξιότιμες κυρίες και κύριοι, αγαπητοί μικροί μας φίλοι, γεια σας και χαρά σας." (Polytiris Kalantzopoulos, present! What performance will we present tonight, my little bird? Esteemed ladies and gentlemen, dear young friends, hello and joy to you.) He concludes by announcing, "Απόψε θα σας διασκεδάσουμε με μία ωραία παράσταση ο πατέρας μου στο Ελλάδα έχεις ταλέντο" (Tonight we will entertain you with a beautiful performance by my father on Greece Got Talent), as the on-screen text 'ΝΕΟ ΕΠΕΙΣΟΔΙΟ #TalentoGR' and 'ΣΚΑΙ HD' remains visible. The segment concludes with the shadow puppets continuing their dance, the judges still engaged, and the audience’s positive energy palpable, with intensified applause and cheering as the male voice says, "Συγχαρητήρια! Περάστε τώρα γρήγορα." (Congratulations! Come quickly now.)

The segment opens with a vibrant theatrical shadow puppet performance projected onto a large screen, featuring a hand-painted backdrop depicting a whimsical parade of cartoonish figures, including a donkey and characters in traditional attire, all set under a red curtain adorned with theatrical masks. The silhouetted shadow figures engage in a lively, story-like sequence, with one figure in a blue dress and red boots moving dynamically. The audio reveals a comedic dialogue between two distinct male voices, identified as the traditional Greek shadow puppet characters Hadjiavatis and Karagiozis. Hadjiavatis, speaking with a deeper, more authoritative voice, calls out to Karagiozis, who responds with a higher-pitched, comedic voice, leading to bursts of audience laughter and applause. The backdrop later updates to include a castle silhouette, suggesting a narrative progression within the performance. The Male Judge in Black T-Shirt reacts with amusement, at one point covering his mouth, while the Female Judge with Platinum Hair observes attentively with a composed expression, and the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer watches with interest, resting his chin on his hand. The audience is seen clapping and reacting positively, their audible laughter and applause punctuating the humorous exchanges. Hadjiavatis then proposes to Karagiozis, stating, "έχουνε φτιάξει το Ελλάδα έχεις ταλέντο μια ωραία εκπομπή και σκέφτηκα να πας εσύ να διαγωνιστείς βρε" (they've made 'Greece Got Talent' a nice show, and I thought you should go compete), praising his dancing and singing skills. Karagiozis excitedly responds, "Θα πάω στο Ελλάδα έχεις ταλέντο; Ναι. Ωχ ρε μανούλα μου" (I'll go to Greece Got Talent? Yes. Oh my goodness.). The on-screen text 'ΝΕΟ ΕΠΕΙΣΟΔΙΟ #TalentoGR' and 'ΣΚΑΙ HD' remains visible throughout, framing the lively and entertaining atmosphere.

The video opens with a vibrant shadow puppet performance projected onto a white screen, featuring two stylized characters engaging in animated Greek dialogue. One puppet, in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs, speaks about "casting" and "singing" with a "beautiful voice," while another, in a blue and gold costume with red boots, responds. A third character, in a green dress, joins the interaction as the first puppet asks "Who is it?" and then introduces her as "Maria Ipakodimos," noting she is "Cretan." The second puppet, later revealing his name as Karagiozis, asks if he should "take out his little name" and then proudly states that girls call him "Zouli," adding humorously that he puts sugar on his name daily to make it sweeter. Karagiozis then declares he is good at "food" and offers to "sing a song." Throughout this lively exchange, the Female Judge with Platinum Hair is seen smiling and laughing, clearly entertained, while the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt also reacts with amusement, at one point covering his mouth. Audience members are shown clapping and enjoying the performance under blue stage lighting, with their laughter audible in response to the puppet's jokes. The backdrop transitions from a silhouetted tree to a castle, and a wider view reveals a hand-painted theatrical backdrop depicting a colorful parade under a red curtain with theatrical masks. On-screen text 'ΝΕΟ ΕΠΕΙΣΟΔΙΟ #TalentoGR' and the 'ΣΚΑΙ HD' logo are visible, framing the talent show context.

The segment opens with a vibrant shadow puppet performance on a white screen, set against a hand-painted theatrical backdrop featuring cartoonish figures in a parade, under a red curtain, later updated with a castle silhouette. Three stylized shadow puppets—one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs, one in a green dress, and one in a blue and gold costume with red boots—engage in animated gestures. A male voice, likely the puppeteer, introduces a Lenten song he wrote, titled "Η Φασολάδα" (Fasolada - Bean Soup), asking "Listen to my song." He then theatrically sings about his love for "Fasolada," making "Brm brm" sounds, while the blue puppet gestures emphatically and dramatically falls or leans, with the voice explaining he's "stirring the beans so they don't stick." Intermittently, the camera cuts to the judges’ panel: the Female Judge with Platinum Hair smiles and laughs, and a female voice, likely hers, exclaims "Bravo! Wonderful!" The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer watches intently, smiling broadly, while the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt reacts with amusement, covering his mouth. Another male voice addresses the performing character as "Karagiozis," praising him as a "beloved hero" and asking him to "tell jokes" to entertain them. The audience, illuminated by blue stage lighting, claps and reacts positively, their applause and laughter audible throughout the lively, theatrical performance, which concludes with the puppets still in motion and the judges engaged.

The video segment opens with a shadow puppet performance projected onto a white screen, set against a hand-painted theatrical backdrop depicting a whimsical parade of cartoonish figures, including a donkey and characters in traditional attire, under a red curtain adorned with theatrical masks. Three stylized shadow puppets—one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs, another in a blue and gold costume with red boots, and a third in a green dress—engage in animated gestures. A male voice, identified as Karagiozis, humorously complains in Greek about others laughing and not eating, forcing him to eat their food. Another male voice, possibly Hadjiavatis, responds, acknowledging that he tells most jokes about food. Karagiozis is then called out by name, with a voice stating, 'I'm waiting for you Karagiozis, I'm waiting for the others to meet you,' followed by 'Hello. Ciao. Ciao.' Another male voice addresses 'Mr. Hadjiavatis,' asking him to bring someone, confirming, 'Indeed, indeed.' The voice then playfully remarks, 'Come on, lucky one! You found a job again! You'll become a jester.' Karagiozis proudly declares, 'This is my job, Hadjiatzaris. To make the world... Bravo. To laugh. To laugh and forget their problems.' The puppet identified as Karagiozis (likely the one in blue and gold) appears to react dynamically to the others, at one point turning away and then returning to interact. The scene shifts to the judges' panel, where the Female Judge with Platinum Hair laughs heartily, the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer watches with interest, and the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt covers his mouth in amusement. The audience is shown clapping and reacting positively under blue stage lighting, their applause audible. Karagiozis then requests, 'Avanti Maestro, a Kalamatianos to dance with my friend Hadjiatzaris,' and lively traditional Greek music, specifically a Kalamatianos, begins to play. The performance continues with the puppets’ movements becoming more expressive, including one figure bending forward dramatically, while the backdrop remains consistent, now featuring a castle silhouette to the right, enhancing the story’s setting. A voice exclaims, 'Bravo, my Karagiozis!' The judges and audience remain engaged, with the Female Judge smiling broadly and the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt visibly entertained, as the music plays. The segment concludes with Karagiozis announcing, 'My dear friends, our performance has come to an end. Goodbye and joy to you!' and a final farewell, 'Goodbye, my little Karagiozis!' as the puppets continue their interaction and the music fades out amidst continued audience clapping, maintaining the playful and theatrical atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: Tasos Georgiou, a large man with a beard, performed a shadow puppet act with his Assistant Performer against a colorful, hand-painted Theatrical Backdrop depicting cartoonish figures and later a castle silhouette. Three stylized Shadow Puppet Characters, including one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs and another in blue and gold with red boots, engaged in animated, argumentative interactions on a white screen, accompanied by distinct male voices, slap sound effects, and upbeat, traditional Greek-sounding instrumental music. The judges, including the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer, the Female Judge with Platinum Hair, and the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt (identified as Giorgos), reacted with amusement and enthusiasm; Giorgos covered his mouth in laughter and later expressed being "πραγματικά συγκινήθηκα" (really got emotional). The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer identified puppets as 'Kolitiris' and the 'Ispaliaras' thrown by Karagiozis at Hatziavatis, with Tasos confirming and clarifying puppet movements. Tasos explained he performed all the voices himself, despite having an assistant backstage. The audience clapped and cheered under blue stage lighting, with dynamic spotlights highlighting the stage, which also featured the 'Ελλάδα Έχει Ταλέντο' logo and a red 'X' symbol. Following the performance, the judges offered praise, with the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer saying "Ευχαριστώ πολύ" (Thank you very much) and Giorgos exclaiming "Μπράβο Τάσο" (Bravo Tasos). Tasos responded with "Να 'στε καλά" (You too/Be well), and Giorgos requested to see backstage, which Tasos accepted with "Βεβαίως" (Of course).

Shadow Puppet Characters.description: Three stylized shadow puppets — one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs, one in a blue and gold costume with red boots, and a third in a green dress — performing animated gestures and interactions on a white screen, engaging in lively, argumentative dialogue with distinct male voices and producing clear slap/hit sound effects.

Theatrical Backdrop.description: A large, hand-painted stage backdrop featuring cartoonish characters in a parade, including a donkey and figures in traditional attire, under a red curtain with theatrical masks; later updated to include a castle silhouette, suggesting a narrative element.

Female Judge with Platinum Hair.description: A woman with short platinum hair, wearing a black patterned top and large hoop earrings, seated at the judges' panel, smiling and laughing in response to the shadow puppet performance, occasionally leaning forward in amusement, and is heard exclaiming "Bravo, Giorgo, bravo!" and later saying "Poli orea" (very nice) with accompanying laughter.

Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer.description: A man with a beard, wearing a grey checkered blazer over a white shirt, initially seated at the judges' panel, then standing and approaching the stage to interact with the performers, gesturing and speaking with enthusiasm in Greek, discussing the performance.

Tasos Georgiou.description: A large man with a beard, wearing a black button-up shirt and jeans, performing on stage in front of a colorful, hand-painted backdrop; identified by on-screen text as 'ΤΑΣΟΣ ΓΕΩΡΓΙΟΥ', holding a shadow puppet rod and manipulating puppets on a white screen, and is heard explaining the sound effects of the shadow puppet theatre, demonstrating a 'slap' sound.

Male Judge in Black T-Shirt.description: A man with a beard, wearing a black t-shirt, seated at the judges' panel, reacting with amusement, at one point covering his mouth with his hand, and later clapping and gesturing in response to the performance, and is heard saying "Thank you very much" and engaging in conversation with the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer.

Audience Members.description: A diverse group of spectators in the audience, shown clapping and reacting positively to the performance, illuminated by blue stage lighting, with some individuals raising their hands in excitement, and are heard cheering enthusiastically alongside celebratory music.

Assistant Performer.description: A man in a dark t-shirt and jeans, standing beside Tasos Georgiou on stage, assisting with the shadow puppet performance by holding and manipulating puppet rods, contributing to the animated movements on the screen.

Stage Screen with Logo.description: A large screen on stage displaying the show’s logo 'ΕΛΛΑΔΑ ΕΧΕΙ ΤΑΛΕΝΤΟ' in bold white letters against a blue, starry background, with a red 'X' symbol visible on the floor, indicating the talent show’s branding and judging system.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a wide shot of the 'Ελλάδα Έχει Ταλέντο' judges' panel, featuring the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer, the Female Judge with Platinum Hair, and the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt, all seated behind a sleek desk with illuminated 'XXX' logos. The audience claps under blue stage lighting, their enthusiastic reactions captured in a close-up. On stage, Tasos Georgiou, a large man with a beard in a black shirt and jeans, begins his shadow puppet performance against a colorful, hand-painted Theatrical Backdrop depicting a parade of cartoonish figures under a red curtain with theatrical masks. He manipulates three stylized Shadow Puppet Characters on a white screen, prompting amusement and interest from the judges, with the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt covering his mouth in laughter and the Female Judge with Platinum Hair smiling broadly. As upbeat, traditional Greek-sounding instrumental music plays, the backdrop updates to a castle silhouette, suggesting narrative progression. After the animated performance, the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer offers a clear "Ευχαριστώ πολύ" (Thank you very much), followed by the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt, identified as Giorgos, praising Tasos with "Μπράβο Τάσο" (Bravo Tasos). Tasos Georgiou responds with "Να 'στε καλά" (You too/Be well), before Giorgos asks if he performed alone or had help. Tasos explains he has an assistant backstage but performs all the voices himself, which Giorgos confirms he did for all the voices heard. Giorgos then expresses genuine emotion, stating he's "πραγματικά συγκινήθηκα" (really got emotional) watching, a sentiment echoed by the Female Judge with Platinum Hair who notes, "Ο Γιώργος συγκινήθηκε" (Giorgos got emotional). Giorgos then requests to see behind the stage, a request Tasos graciously accepts with "Βεβαίως" (Of course). The segment concludes with a wide-angle shot of the lively stage, judges' desk, and audience, as the upbeat music swells with renewed clapping and cheering.

The video opens with an upbeat, rhythmic musical score as the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt, seated at the judges' panel, reacts with amusement, at one point covering his mouth. The scene quickly transitions to a wide shot of the stage, revealing the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer and the Female Judge with Platinum Hair, both engaged and smiling, while the audience claps and reacts positively under blue stage lighting. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer asks, "Παίξε μας και κάτι, ε;" (Play something for us, eh?), and then greets Tasos Georgiou, "Γεια σου κιόλας, Τάσο." (Hello there, Tasos.), to which Tasos replies, "Ευχαριστώ πολύ." (Thank you very much.). The judge then addresses the Assistant Performer, "Γιώργο, παίξε μας και κάτι." (George, play something for us.), and asks, "Πώς είσαι;" (How are you?), as Tasos responds, "Ωραίο θα 'ναι." (It will be nice.). The focus shifts to Tasos Georgiou, a large man in a black button-up shirt, interacting with the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer, who stands up and approaches the performance area. Tasos holds a shadow puppet rod, and together with the Assistant Performer, they manipulate shadow puppets on a white screen. The backdrop features a hand-painted castle and later transitions to a parade scene with cartoonish figures under a red curtain with theatrical masks. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer identifies one puppet, stating, "Αυτό λοιπόν είναι ο Κολιτήρης." (So this is Kolitiris.), which Tasos confirms, "Ναι, ο Κολιτήρης." (Yes, Kolitiris.). The judge explains the puppet's movement, "Τον βάζουμε και να πατήσει κάτω, δέβαια." (We also make him step down, of course.), and Tasos acknowledges, "Α, πατάει εδώ, ναι." (Ah, he steps here, yes.), followed by the judge's instruction, "Ακριβώς. Τον ακουμπάμε στον πανί και τον κουνάμε." (Exactly. We touch him to the screen and move him.). The judge praises their effort, "Είναι μια πολύ καλή προσπάθεια για πρώτη φορά." (It's a very good effort for a first time.), to which Tasos responds with a surprised "Αλήθεια;" (Really?) and a laugh. The judge then urges, "Πες κάτι, Γιώργο, πες κάτι!" (Say something, George, say something!), and after a brief, unclear response from George, Tasos exclaims, "Κοίτα, εγώ αυτό που είδα μ' άρεσε πολύ. Λέω να πω... Ναι!" (Look, I really liked what I saw. I'm going to say... Yes!), prompting loud cheers and applause from the audience. The judge then asks, "Αυτό τι είναι;" (What is this?), and explains, "Αυτό λοιπόν είναι ο Ισπαλιάρας που ρίχνει ο Καραγκιόζης στον Χατζηαβάτη." (So this is the 'Ispaliaras' that Karagiozis throws at Hatziavatis.), with Tasos asking for clarification, "Πώς γίνεται αυτό;" (How does this happen?). The judge suggests, "Ε, μπορούμε να το δείξουμε." (Uh, we can show it.), adding, "Μπορεί να το δείξει και ο βοηθός μας, εφόσον ήτανε κι αυτός μαζί μας." (Our assistant can also show it, since he was with us too.), which Tasos affirms, "Ναι, ναι, ναι." (Yes, yes, yes.). The shadow puppets, including one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs and another in a blue and gold costume with red boots, perform animated gestures, suggesting a narrative dialogue or confrontation, as the judge concludes by naming another puppet, "Καραγκιόζης." The judges and audience continue to react with amusement and applause, with dynamic spotlights highlighting the stage and performers, creating a vibrant and engaging atmosphere throughout the performance.

The segment opens with Tasos Georgiou and his Assistant Performer setting up a shadow puppet performance on a stage featuring a hand-painted Theatrical Backdrop. Shadow Puppet Characters, including one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs and another in blue and gold, engage in animated interactions on a white screen, their playful, argumentative dialogue delivered by distinct male voices, punctuated by clear slap/hit sound effects. The judges observe intently; the Female Judge with Platinum Hair laughs and leans forward, while the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt covers his mouth in surprise. A male voice, likely Tasos Georgiou, explains the sound effects of the shadow puppet theatre, demonstrating the 'slap' sound. The Female Judge with Platinum Hair exclaims "Bravo, Giorgo, bravo!" with enthusiasm, followed by the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt saying "Thank you very much." The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer stands and approaches the stage, gesturing enthusiastically, then returns to the panel. Upbeat, celebratory music swells as the Audience Members clap and cheer, some raising their hands in excitement. The judges continue their positive reactions, with the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer and Male Judge in Black T-Shirt conversing in Greek, and the Female Judge with Platinum Hair adding "Poli orea" (very nice) with accompanying laughter. The stage prominently displays the 'ΕΛΛΑΔΑ ΕΧΕΙ ΤΑΛΕΝΤΟ' logo and a red 'X' symbol, underscoring the talent competition setting.

event_blocks.summary: Tasos Georgiou, a large man with a beard, stood center stage before a colorful theatrical backdrop, manipulating shadow puppets on a white screen with a rod, illuminated by blue lighting. He spoke of Karagiozis shadow puppet theater as a vital tradition, a "small world" and "proper theater" that predates cinema and television, aiming to preserve and spread this art form by training new students and establishing a permanent venue. The judges, including the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer, Female Judge with Platinum Hair, and Male Judge in Black T-Shirt, observed attentively. The Female Judge with Platinum Hair inquired about Karagiozis museums and performances, which Tasos confirmed exist, and complimented his skill as a "good Karagiozis player." Later, the Female Judge praised his performance for its humor, tradition, and ability to evoke childhood memories, to which Tasos responded with thanks as the audience applauded. The Male Judge in Black T-Shirt expressed appreciation, questioning if such traditions resonate with younger generations, while the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer agreed, focusing on Tasos as a "new, modern person who stubbornly tries to keep..." The judges ultimately gave "yes" votes, with the Female Judge expressing a desire for more "Karagiozis" performances and the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt noting three "yeses." Discussion ensued about specific puppet figures, like "Μαρία," described as a "plump woman" in a "green dress." The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer announced a surprise "next phase" where all would appear as figures, concluding with anticipation for seeing the remaining figures and Tasos again, to which the Female Judge responded with thanks. The segment ended with the show's logo 'ΕΛΛΑΔΑ ΕΧΕΙ ΤΑΛΕΝΤΟ' displayed, a red 'X' on the floor, and a transition to a dramatic orchestral theme, with upbeat celebratory music playing earlier during applause and cheers.

Theatrical Backdrop.description: A large, hand-painted stage backdrop featuring cartoonish characters in a parade, including a donkey and figures in traditional attire, under a red curtain with theatrical masks.

Female Judge with Platinum Hair.description: A woman with short platinum hair, wearing a black patterned top and large hoop earrings, seated at the judges' panel, speaking and gesturing with her hands, appearing engaged and expressive. She interjects 'What, me?' when 'Μαρία' is mentioned, and later thanks the performer and judges with 'Thank you very much.'

Stage Screen with Logo.description: A large screen on stage displaying the show’s logo 'ΕΛΛΑΔΑ ΕΧΕΙ ΤΑΛΕΝΤΟ' in bold white letters against a blue, starry background, with a red 'X' symbol visible on the floor, indicating the talent show’s branding and judging system.

Tasos Georgiou.description: A large man with a beard, wearing a black button-up shirt and jeans, performing on stage in front of a colorful, hand-painted backdrop depicting a parade of cartoonish figures; holding a shadow puppet rod and manipulating puppets on a white screen.

Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer.description: A man with a beard, wearing a grey checkered blazer over a white shirt, seated at the judges' panel, clapping and later speaking with animated hand gestures, showing focused interest. He speaks in Greek, explaining the nature of the puppets as human caricatures, mentions a figure reminding him of 'Μαρία,' announces that 'you will all appear as figures,' calls it 'a surprise,' and expresses eagerness for the next phase, concluding with 'I thank you. Be well. Goodbye.'

Male Judge in Black T-Shirt.description: A man with a beard, wearing a black t-shirt, seated at the judges' panel, speaking and gesturing with his hands, displaying an expressive and engaged demeanor. He exclaims 'Ah!' upon hearing about appearing as figures and later says 'Perfect' in response to a surprise announcement.

Audience Members.description: A diverse group of spectators in the audience, shown clapping and reacting positively to the performance, illuminated by blue stage lighting, with some individuals raising their hands in excitement, their applause and cheers becoming prominent towards the end of the segment.

Shadow Puppet Characters.description: Three stylized shadow puppets — one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs, one in a blue and gold costume with red boots, and a third in a green dress — performing animated gestures and interactions on a white screen, suggesting a narrative dialogue or confrontation.

Assistant Performer.description: A man in a dark t-shirt and jeans, standing beside Tasos Georgiou on stage, assisting with the shadow puppet performance by holding and manipulating puppet rods, contributing to the animated movements on the screen.

raw_segments: The video opens with Tasos Georgiou, a large man with a beard, wearing a black button-up shirt and jeans, standing center stage in front of a colorful, hand-painted theatrical backdrop depicting a parade of cartoonish figures under a red curtain with theatrical masks. He holds a shadow puppet rod, manipulating puppets on a white screen, as if performing or having just performed. The stage is illuminated with blue lighting, and a red 'X' symbol marks the floor, indicating the talent show setting. On-screen text 'ΝΕΟ ΕΠΕΙΣΟΔΙΟ #TalentoGR' and 'ΣΚΑΙ HD' are visible. As Tasos speaks in a clear, engaging male voice, he expresses nostalgia for childhood, describing the Karagiozis shadow puppet theater as "a small world back there" and "a proper theater." He explains that many admired old actors started as Karagiozis assistants, emphasizing the art form's rich history from a time before cinema or television, when it was the primary entertainment, even in Athens. He mentions the existence of many permanent Karagiozis theaters and articulates a mission to preserve and spread the tradition, hoping to train new students and eventually establish a permanent venue for performances and learning for children. The camera cuts to the judges' panel, showing the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer, Female Judge with Platinum Hair, and Male Judge in Black T-Shirt observing attentively. The Female Judge with Platinum Hair, wearing a black patterned top and large hoop earrings, then speaks in a distinct female voice, asking if there's a Karagiozis museum and if performances are held there. Tasos confirms there are two museums where performances take place. The Female Judge then compliments Tasos, stating, "Tasos, I liked what I saw. I think you are a good Karagiozis player." Throughout this, background music plays, creating an engaging atmosphere.

The video opens with Tasos Georgiou, a large man with a beard wearing a black button-up shirt and jeans, poised on stage before a colorful, hand-painted theatrical backdrop depicting a parade of cartoonish figures, holding a shadow puppet rod. The scene cuts to the Female Judge with Platinum Hair, who animatedly speaks and gestures, praising the performance for its humor, consistency with tradition, and its touching ability to evoke childhood memories of Karagiozis. The camera returns to Tasos Georgiou, who stands ready to begin his shadow puppet performance, and after the judge's comments, he is heard saying "Thank you very much" as the audience claps. The judges' panel is shown, with the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer clapping, the Female Judge with Platinum Hair listening intently, and the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt observing. Tasos Georgiou then begins his shadow puppet performance, manipulating the rod with focused movements. The Male Judge in Black T-Shirt is shown again, addressing Tasos by name and speaking with gestures, expressing his genuine appreciation for the performance. He elaborates on the nostalgic elements of the past, like grandmothers knitting doilies, but thoughtfully questions whether such traditions truly resonate with or interest younger generations. The stage is illuminated with blue lighting, highlighting the vibrant backdrop, as the camera alternates between Tasos Georgiou’s focused performance and the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt’s commentary. The audience is visible in the background, reacting with interest and occasional applause, contributing to the lively ambiance, while the on-screen text 'ΝΕΟ ΕΠΕΙΣΟΔΙΟ #TalentoGR' and the 'ΣΚΑΙ HD' logo are consistently displayed.

The video segment opens with the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt speaking animatedly in Greek, gesturing with his hands while seated at the judges' panel. He expresses his enjoyment of the performance, stating, "I liked it, I would like to follow it," and emphasizes that it's not just about the art of shadow theater but about the performer's "own art, your own talent," before concluding with "Bravo, Tasos." The scene then cuts to Tasos Georgiou on stage, a large man with a beard, standing before a colorful Theatrical Backdrop depicting a parade of cartoonish figures, holding a shadow puppet rod and preparing to perform. Tasos responds to the judge with "Thank you very much," as the audience claps. The camera alternates between close-ups of the judges, who react with focused attention. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer is seen clapping and then speaks with emphasis in Greek, agreeing with the previous judge. He explains that from a certain point, he stopped critically evaluating the scenario, voice, or movement, and instead focused on the performer, stating that what truly remains with him from Tasos's performance is that he is "a new, modern person who stubbornly tries to keep..." The stage is illuminated with blue lighting, and the audience is visible in the background, reacting with interest and applause. Tasos Georgiou continues his performance, manipulating shadow puppets on a white screen, with the Stage Screen with Logo visible in the background, reinforcing the talent show setting. The judges remain attentive, with the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt frequently gesturing as if explaining or commenting on the act, contributing to an atmosphere of anticipation and engagement.

The video opens with Tasos Georgiou, a large man with a beard in a black button-up shirt and jeans, standing on stage in front of a colorful, hand-painted theatrical backdrop depicting a parade of cartoonish figures, holding a shadow puppet rod. The scene cuts to the judges' panel, where the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer speaks animatedly in Greek, praising Tasos's talent and giving a "big yes" vote, stating, "you surely must have talent." The Female Judge with Platinum Hair listens intently, occasionally responding with expressive hand movements, while the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt also engages. The audience, illuminated by blue stage lighting, reacts with applause and visible excitement, some raising their hands, as upbeat celebratory music begins. The camera returns to Tasos Georgiou, who begins manipulating shadow puppets on a white screen, creating animated figures in blue, gold, and green costumes that interact dynamically. The backdrop features a red curtain with theatrical masks. The Female Judge with Platinum Hair then speaks in Greek, also giving a "yes" and expressing her desire for Tasos to return to the stage to perform more "Karagiozis" shadow puppet shows. The Male Judge in Black T-Shirt adds his "yes" vote in Greek, noting Tasos has received three "yeses," and asks if the "Maria" puppet figure was made specifically for today. Tasos Georgiou responds in Greek, confirming it was not. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer then describes the puppet as a "plump woman" in a "green dress," continuing to discuss the "Karagiozis" figures. Throughout, the judges continue to react with interest, clapping and discussing the performance, while the audience remains engaged, clapping and smiling, with the show’s logo 'ΕΛΛΑΔΑ ΕΧΕΙ ΤΑΛΕΝΤΟ' displayed on the stage screen behind the judges.

The segment opens with a wide shot of the 'ΕΛΛΑΔΑ ΕΧΕΙ ΤΑΛΕΝΤΟ' judges' panel, where the Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer, the Female Judge with Platinum Hair, and the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt are seated behind a sleek black desk with 'XXX' branding. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer speaks in Greek, explaining that the puppets represent people, essentially caricatures close to human form, and notes a specific figure reminded him of "Μαρία." The Female Judge with Platinum Hair interjects, "What, me?" and later thanks him as he prepares for the next phase. On stage, Tasos Georgiou, a large man with a beard, wearing a black button-up shirt, performs shadow puppetry in front of a colorful, hand-painted Theatrical Backdrop, manipulating animated Shadow Puppet Characters on a white screen, with an Assistant Performer briefly assisting. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer announces that "you will all appear as figures," prompting an "Ah!" from the Male Judge in Black T-Shirt, who later exclaims "Perfect" when the first judge calls it "a surprise." The camera alternates between close-ups of the judges reacting, with the Female Judge with Platinum Hair speaking and gesturing expressively, and wide shots of Tasos Georgiou's performance, which includes puppets in blue, blue and gold, and green outfits suggesting a narrative. The Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer concludes by saying, "We eagerly await the next phase to see the remaining figures and you again," to which the Female Judge with Platinum Hair responds with a heartfelt "Thank you very much." He replies, "I thank you. Be well. Goodbye," as the audience begins to clap and cheer positively, some raising their hands in excitement, accompanied by upbeat, celebratory background music. The segment concludes with a full-screen display of the show’s logo 'ΕΛΛΑΔΑ ΕΧΕΙ ΤΑΛΕΝΤΟ' against a blue, starry background, with a red 'X' symbol on the floor, as the music transitions to a dramatic, orchestral theme.

global_entity_library.Theatrical Backdrop: A large, hand-painted stage backdrop featuring cartoonish characters in a parade, including a donkey and figures in traditional attire, under a red curtain with theatrical masks.
global_entity_library.Female Judge with Platinum Hair: A woman with short platinum hair, wearing a black patterned top and large hoop earrings, seated at the judges' panel, speaking and gesturing with her hands, appearing engaged and expressive. She interjects 'What, me?' when 'Μαρία' is mentioned, and later thanks the performer and judges with 'Thank you very much.'
global_entity_library.Male Judge in Grey Checkered Blazer: A man with a beard, wearing a grey checkered blazer over a white shirt, seated at the judges' panel, clapping and later speaking with animated hand gestures, showing focused interest. He speaks in Greek, explaining the nature of the puppets as human caricatures, mentions a figure reminding him of 'Μαρία,' announces that 'you will all appear as figures,' calls it 'a surprise,' and expresses eagerness for the next phase, concluding with 'I thank you. Be well. Goodbye.'
global_entity_library.Tasos Georgiou: A large man with a beard, wearing a black button-up shirt and jeans, performing on stage in front of a colorful, hand-painted backdrop depicting a parade of cartoonish figures; holding a shadow puppet rod and manipulating puppets on a white screen.
global_entity_library.Male Judge in Black T-Shirt: A man with a beard, wearing a black t-shirt, seated at the judges' panel, speaking and gesturing with his hands, displaying an expressive and engaged demeanor. He exclaims 'Ah!' upon hearing about appearing as figures and later says 'Perfect' in response to a surprise announcement.
global_entity_library.Audience Members: A diverse group of spectators in the audience, shown clapping and reacting positively to the performance, illuminated by blue stage lighting, with some individuals raising their hands in excitement, their applause and cheers becoming prominent towards the end of the segment.
global_entity_library.Shadow Puppet Characters: Three stylized shadow puppets — one in a blue outfit with polka-dotted legs, one in a blue and gold costume with red boots, and a third in a green dress — performing animated gestures and interactions on a white screen, suggesting a narrative dialogue or confrontation.
global_entity_library.Assistant Performer: A man in a dark t-shirt and jeans, standing beside Tasos Georgiou on stage, assisting with the shadow puppet performance by holding and manipulating puppet rods, contributing to the animated movements on the screen.
global_entity_library.Stage Screen with Logo: A large screen on stage displaying the show’s logo 'ΕΛΛΑΔΑ ΕΧΕΙ ΤΑΛΕΝΤΟ' in bold white letters against a blue, starry background, with a red 'X' symbol visible on the floor, indicating the talent show’s branding and judging system.
